claimText: The Hazardous and Other Wastes Amendment Rules, 2025 introduce an Extended Producer Responsibility (EPR) framework specifically for scrap of non-ferrous metals such as aluminium, copper, and zinc.
evidenceExcerpt: Introduces comprehensive Extended Producer Responsibility (EPR) framework for Scrap of Non-Ferrous Metals (including aluminium, copper and zinc and its alloys)
examUse: Statement-based question on the scope and applicability of the 2025 Amendment Rules.
distractorHooks: Ferrous metals like iron and steel

claimText: Under the 2025 Amendment Rules, the recycling target for producers of non-ferrous metal scrap progressively increases from 10% in 2026-2027 to 75% by 2032-2033.
evidenceExcerpt: Targets: Every producer shall be responsible for recycling target structure, which progressively increases from 10% in 2026-2027 to 75% by 2032-2033.
examUse: Statement-based question testing specific quantitative targets and timelines of the EPR framework.
distractorHooks: A flat recycling target of 50% from the first year

claimText: The Central Pollution Control Board (CPCB) is the designated authority responsible for generating Extended Producer Responsibility (EPR) certificates for non-ferrous metal scrap.
evidenceExcerpt: Central Pollution Control Board: Shall generate an EPR certificate
examUse: Institution-feature question or correct-pair matching regarding regulatory authorities in waste management.
distractorHooks: Ministry of Environment, Forest and Climate Change (MoEFCC) directly

claimText: Extended Producer Responsibility (EPR) certificates generated under the 2025 Amendment Rules are valid for two years from the end of the financial year in which they are generated.
evidenceExcerpt: Validity of the EPR certificate: Two years from the end of financial year when generated.
examUse: Statement-based question on compliance mechanisms and certificate validity periods.
distractorHooks: Valid for one year from the date of issue
Valid for five years from the end of the financial year
